---
applyTo: "tests/features/**/*.feature"
---

# Feature-file authoring rules (STRICT)

When you create or modify a `.feature` file in this repository, follow these
rules. Violations will be rejected by `scripts/validate-test-structure.js`.

## Location

- Allowed path pattern:
  `tests/features/<module-slug>/<story-slug>.feature`
- `<module-slug>` is derived at runtime from Jira/Zephyr metadata (Components →
  Labels → Zephyr Folder → Story Title) via `classifyModule()` in
  `src/tools/featureFile.writer.js`. **No fixed module taxonomy is hardcoded.**
- `<story-slug>` is derived from the story title via `slugFile()` in the same
  file (e.g. `employee-login-and-pim-add-employee.feature`).
- Forbidden: `tests/features/_unsorted/`, `tests/features/misc/`,
  story-keyed folders, person-keyed folders, date-keyed folders.

## Structure

- Top-of-file managed banner (auto-generated by
  `src/tools/featureFile.writer.js` — keep it intact):
  ```
  # =============================================================================
  # Module : <module-slug>
  # ...
  # =============================================================================
  Feature: <Module Display Name>
    Background:
      Given <APP_NAME> is accessible at the configured base URL
  ```
- Each story occupies a delimited block:
  ```
  # ─── BEGIN STORY <KEY> ───
  ...scenarios...
  # ─── END STORY <KEY> ───
  ```
- Every scenario MUST have these tags (at minimum):
  `@<PROJECT>-T<id>`  `@<storyKey-lowercase>`  `@<module-slug>`
  plus design-technique tags (`@happy-path`, `@boundary`, `@security`, ...).

## When module is unknown

Do NOT write to `_unsorted/`. Either:
- Re-read the story's Jira Components, Labels, Zephyr folder, and title and let
  the classifier derive the module, or
- Set `opts.module` override when calling `syncStoryFeature()`.

The writer (`featureFile.writer.js`) logs a warning and falls back to
`general/` — do not suppress warnings without fixing the root cause.
